description: Control message types. The stream field records which stream a message is sent on: "Control" is the control stream (§3.3), "Request" a bidirectional request stream. "First" means the message MUST be the first on a new request stream. Reserved codepoints carry no stream and MUST be rejected.

entries.name: RESERVED_SETUP_V00
entries.notes: SETUP for version 00. moqtail-ts maps this to a ReservedSetupV00 variant instead of rejecting it.

entries.name: RESERVED_CLIENT_SETUP_V10
entries.notes: CLIENT_SETUP for version <= 10. moqtail-ts maps this to a ReservedClientSetupV10 variant instead of rejecting it.

entries.name: RESERVED_SERVER_SETUP_V10
entries.notes: SERVER_SETUP for version <= 10. moqtail-ts maps this to a ReservedServerSetupV10 variant instead of rejecting it.

entries.name: RESERVED_CLIENT_SETUP_V16
entries.notes: CLIENT_SETUP in version <= 16. moqtail-ts still carries a live CLIENT_SETUP here.

entries.name: RESERVED_SERVER_SETUP_V16
entries.notes: SERVER_SETUP in version <= 16. moqtail-ts still carries a live SERVER_SETUP here.

entries.name: SUBSCRIBE_OK
entries.notes: Remains a distinct message with its own body; not folded into REQUEST_OK.

entries.name: PUBLISH_OK
entries.notes: Table 5 keeps this codepoint but points it at Section 10.5, REQUEST_OK: it is an alias, not a message with its own body.

entries.name: FETCH_OK
entries.notes: Remains a distinct message with its own body; not folded into REQUEST_OK.

not_in_draft.name: UNSUBSCRIBE
not_in_draft.notes: Requests are cancelled by resetting the request stream (§3.3.2), not by a message.

not_in_draft.name: FETCH_CANCEL
not_in_draft.notes: Requests are cancelled by resetting the request stream (§3.3.2), not by a message.

not_in_draft.name: UNSUBSCRIBE_NAMESPACE
not_in_draft.notes: Requests are cancelled by closing the request stream (§3.3.2), not by a message.

local_extensions.name: SWITCH
local_extensions.notes: moqtail's own track-switching research extension. Not in the draft, and deliberately kept: draft-18 defines no registry for control message types, so there is no sanctioned range for it. Documented rather than removed. 0x22 is unassigned in Table 5 but live in neighbouring registries (GROUP_ORDER parameter, DEFAULT_PUBLISHER_GROUP_ORDER property), so it is a plausible future assignment.
